events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the inspection result directly to `/app/answer.json`, treating the record sequence against API RP 686-style alignment practice: soft foot first, piping connected before final alignment, tolerance verified, hot growth offsets applied.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'

  \"references\": [
    \"API RP 686 - machinery installation and alignment practices\",
    \"OEM thermal growth/cold offset chart\",
    \"Job card 1800-rpm alignment tolerance table\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"

drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:43 ..
/bin/sh: 31: jq: not found

events.label: assistant
events.text: `jq` is not installed in this container, so I’m doing a lightweight JSON parse check with the available runtime before closing this out.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/tmp/answer-json-check.txt && sed -n '1,120p' /tmp/answer-json-check.txt"
